rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MORTGAGE LENDER SERVICES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2023 line 1. HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS 1081-1083-1085-1087 Mississippi Street Homeowners Associations' Claim is granted in full, plus interest rate at 12%. HOA to submit a proposed order with the total amount due through August 1, 2023 at the time of the hearing. Olusoji Fanoiki's claim is granted to the extent any amounts remain after the satisfaction of the HOA claim. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SPALDING VS. VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF GENERAL DEMURRER OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SPECIAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF GENERAL DEMURRER OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SPECIAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each cause of action in clear and concise language, clearly identify the defendants against whom a particular cause of action is alleged. Each element of each cause of action must be supported by facts. The Court notes that outrageous conduct in support of the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is sufficiently stated. All amendments must be in bold font. Plaintiff shall also not re-allege the parts of the complaint that have been stricken per CCP 425.16. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SPALDING VS. VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to strike the entire cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is DENIED. Motion is granted as to (1) the following part of paragraph 77 of the First Amended Complaint: " ...including now the wrongful and aggressive eviction of Plaintiff, ..." and (2) paragraphs 59, 62, 63, 67; exhibits A39, A57, B11, B14a, B18 B30 to the extent any claims asserted in the complaint arise out of the allegations contained therein. The Court notes that these paragraphs are incorporated into each cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FERRY VS. MATTHEW RICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT ARTHUR GROFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on August 1, 2023. Defendant to file, serve and provide a courtesy copy of medical evidence regarding disability. A note from a doctor confirming diagnosis is sufficient. If defendant intends on submitting more detailed records (in lieu of a note referenced above), such records may be submitted without filing for in- Camera review by 9:00 a.m. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
